stimulus.title: Feed Ts Pagination Reader Ux case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 9498 during an out-of-order Promise and AbortSignal rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove deterministic ordering, signed cursors, keyset directions, changing data, and accessible navigation; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide keyboard-operable next and previous navigation, focus continuity, result context, URL state, reflow, and no infinite-scroll dependency.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Cursor APIs require endless scrolling in the UI.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide keyboard-operable next and previous navigation, focus continuity, result context, URL state, reflow, and no infinite-scroll dependency. Artifact under review: stable accessible entry timeline.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feed Ts Pagination Reader Ux, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Feed Ts Pagination Reader Ux record says stable accessible entry timeline looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Feed Ts Pagination Reader Ux.

For separate evidence from confidence, cast one convenient XML object as a feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, source and emitted revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, entity and dangerous-key limits, unknown runtime validation, Fetch stream and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL client, Promise settlement, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as feed-service proof. This does not satisfy Feed Ts Pagination Reader Ux for stable accessible entry timeline.

steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Ts Pagination Reader Ux: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review TypeScript team handles case 1403 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove deterministic ordering, signed cursors, keyset directions, changing data, and accessible navigation; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, migrations, lockfile, package, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one emitted artifact, package, container, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Provide keyboard-operable next and previous navigation, focus continuity, result context, URL state, reflow, and no infinite-scroll dependency.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide keyboard-operable next and previous navigation, focus continuity, result context, URL state, reflow, and no infinite-scroll dependency.
